classification:
Classification: Medium
Rationale: Standard multi-surface build with core user-facing and admin panels, standard database operations, and basic third-party integrations.
risks:
Top risks:
- Scope creep during feature iteration
- Minor browser compatibility issues
- Delayed API design sign-off

Mitigations:
- Strictly lock the MVP deliverables list before design phase
- Implement continuous integration and browser layout testing
- Establish clear API contract definitions early on
document:
## Deliverables
- High-fidelity UI mockups and design assets
- Component-based frontend architecture
- Backend REST API integration

## Suggested Stack
- Frontend: Vue 3 + PrimeVue + Pinia
- Backend: Laravel API with Sanctum auth
- Database: PostgreSQL

## Timeline
- Week 1: Design review & database migration definitions
- Week 2-3: Frontend-backend feature implementation
- Week 4: End-to-end testing, bug fixing, and launch readiness

## Out of Scope
- Native iOS/Android app wrappers
- Custom machine learning prediction model training

classification:
Classification: High
Rationale: High complexity due to real-time synchronization requirements, low latency messaging patterns, and complex backend data pipelines.
risks:
Top risks:
- High-concurrency performance bottlenecks
- Websocket disconnection handling and message delivery guarantees
- Complex edge-case handling in data replication

Mitigations:
- Run early load tests on the WebSocket server connection
- implement auto-reconnection and local caching of unsent messages
- Define a clear failover database protocol
document:
## Deliverables
- Real-time data pipeline architecture document
- Scalable WebSocket gateway service
- Client-side offline-first state synchronization engine

## Suggested Stack
- Frontend: React + Redux Toolkit
- Backend: Node.js (Express/Fastify) + Socket.io
- Database: Redis + PostgreSQL

## Timeline
- Week 1-2: Architecture design and setup of WebSocket server
- Week 3-4: Local state synchronization logic & client integration
- Week 5: Multi-client load testing and optimization
- Week 6: Handover documentation & final deployment

## Out of Scope
- Supporting old browsers (e.g. Internet Explorer)
- Manual database migration script generation for legacy platforms

classification:
Classification: Low
Rationale: Single-page application, standard CRUD forms, and pre-packaged UI libraries with minimal external API requirements.
risks:
Top risks:
- Form validation errors and edge cases
- Styling inconsistencies across responsive layout breakpoints
- Slow page loading due to unoptimized assets

Mitigations:
- Use Yup or Zod schema validation on all inputs
- Strictly adhere to responsive grid helper classes
- Compress all image assets and enable bundle splitting
document:
## Deliverables
- Responsive user interface matching branding guidelines
- Lightweight relational database structure
- Functional email alert and notification trigger system

## Suggested Stack
- Frontend: Vue.js + TailwindCSS
- Backend: Laravel / PHP 8
- Database: SQLite / MySQL
- Mail service: Mailgun / SendGrid

## Timeline
- Week 1: UI template creation & database table creation
- Week 2: Form submission handling & email service configuration
- Week 3: Manual QA testing, styling tweaks, and production release

## Out of Scope
- High-availability database cluster setup
- Multi-lingual translation (localized copy)

classification:
Classification: Medium-High
Rationale: Involves payment gateway integrations, cart state management, and inventory tracking where state accuracy is critical.
risks:
Top risks:
- Payment processing security and PCI compliance concerns
- Race conditions in product inventory deduction
- Multi-currency conversion precision issues

Mitigations:
- Delegate payment collection to Stripe Elements / Hosted checkout pages
- Utilize database transaction locks during item booking/deduction
- Use high-precision numeric types (BigDecimal) for all pricing calculations
document:
## Deliverables
- Fully integrated shopping cart checkout workflow
- Webhook listeners for real-time payment status updates
- Admin panel for inventory management and order history

## Suggested Stack
- Frontend: Next.js + TailwindCSS
- Backend: Stripe API integration + Laravel API
- Database: MySQL with InnoDB engine

## Timeline
- Week 1: Cart logic & Stripe webhook configuration
- Week 2: Inventory transaction logic & database updates
- Week 3: Payment testing (Stripe sandbox) and edge-case handling
- Week 4: Final deployment and soft launch

## Out of Scope
- Direct handling of raw credit card data on our servers
- Physical logistics shipment tracking provider integration

classification:
Classification: Medium
Rationale: Focuses on internal dashboarding, visualization of key metrics, and custom REST API integrations for business data ingestion.
risks:
Top risks:
- Slow loading dashboard charts with large datasets
- Access control leaks (unprivileged users seeing sensitive data)
- Intermittent downtime of external source APIs

Mitigations:
- Aggregate database records prior to rendering or use read replicas
- Enforce strict Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) middleware
- Cache external API data locally with a short TTL
document:
## Deliverables
- Interactive dashboard charts and table layouts
- CSV / PDF export feature for all reports
- Secure OAuth2 single sign-on (SSO) login flow

## Suggested Stack
- Frontend: Vue 3 + Chart.js + PrimeVue
- Backend: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy
- Auth: Auth0 or Okta integration

## Timeline
- Week 1: Schema design & authentication setup
- Week 2: REST API integrations & data aggregation jobs
- Week 3: Dashboard charts & export features
- Week 4: QA, performance tuning, and launch

## Out of Scope
- Live stream video analytics dashboarding
- Automatic translation of generated reports into multiple formats
